meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits for Students: Debugging Code Like a Pro
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how to use SaaS starter kits, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build professional-grade applications as a beginner student.
content.answer_block: When you cannot debug your code, you are likely fighting architectural complexity rather than syntax. A SaaS starter kit provides a battle-tested foundation, offloading boilerplate to pre-configured patterns. By pairing this with AI agents like Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform debugging from an endless manual struggle into an automated, guided process.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen filled with red error lines, the late-night silence amplified by the frustrating realization that your application has collapsed under its own weight. It is the classic 'student trap': you have the vision, but the technical debt of building from scratch is paralyzing your progress. You feel like you are writing code in the dark, hoping something sticks.

But here is the truth: professional engineers rarely build from a blank slate. They use robust, opinionated starter kits that handle the heavy lifting of authentication, database schemas, and API routing. By leveraging AI-driven tooling, you can bypass the 'debugging purgatory' and focus on shipping features that actually matter for your portfolio.

steps.title: Select a Robust Foundation
steps.description: Choose a starter kit that aligns with your tech stack, ensuring it includes pre-configured CI/CD and type safety. This eliminates the 'it works on my machine' debugging nightmare.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current repository structure and suggest a SaaS starter kit template that supports Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe, focusing on modularity.
steps.claude_code_command: ls -R | claude ask 'Review my project structure and identify the best starter kit boilerplate to migrate into.'

steps.title: Automate Dependency Audits
steps.description: Use AI to scan for version conflicts before they manifest as runtime errors. A clean dependency graph is the first step toward a bug-free build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a deep dependency audit of my package.json. Identify any conflicting versions or deprecated packages that could cause silent runtime failures.

steps.title: Map Your Error Surface
steps.description: Before fixing individual bugs, use Claude Code to map out the data flow. Understanding the 'why' behind the error is more important than the 'how' of the fix.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Trace the data flow from the authentication middleware to the database query. Identify where the null-pointer exception is most likely occurring.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Trace the execution path for the login function and point out potential failure states.'

steps.title: Implement Defensive Coding Patterns
steps.description: Refactor your logic to be fail-safe. By wrapping critical operations in standard error-handling wrappers, you prevent minor bugs from crashing the entire app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply a standardized error handling wrapper to all my API routes in the /app/api folder to ensure consistent logging and graceful degradation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor all files in /app/api to include global error boundaries'

steps.title: Automated Integration Testing
steps.description: Don't test manually. Use AI-generated scripts to simulate user behavior, ensuring that your core features remain stable during your iterative learning process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Playwright test script that simulates a full user sign-up flow, including database verification and error state handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a new test file in /tests that verifies the user authentication flow'

pro_tips: Always treat your console errors as data points, not failures; feed them directly into the AI to identify patterns.
Use a 'Feature Flag' approach even in student projects to isolate new, buggy code from your stable, working core.
Prioritize 'Type-First' development; if your code isn't type-safe, the debugger will always be one step ahead of you.
Audit your AI prompts: if the fix doesn't work, ask the AI to explain the logic behind its proposed solution before applying it.
Keep your git commits granular; if a debug session goes south, you should only ever be three minutes away from a working state.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully integrated your starter kit, deploy your application to Vercel or Railway to experience the full CI/CD loop in a production environment.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework, the tools, and the mindset to stop fighting your code and start architecting solutions. Stop guessing and start building with professional-grade precision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
